name: Guided Buying Agent
tagline: Compliant purchasing for every employee - in the tools your requesters already use.

intro.description: When the procurement process is painful, requesters bypass it. The Guided Buying Agent meets requesters in email or Microsoft Teams and creates a compliant Purchase Request (PR) in 30 seconds - not 20 minutes of portal navigation.

calculator.headline: Hard savings recovered from compliance
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.maverick*.75*(e.savingsRate/100);return[{label:"Maverick share recaptured",value:75,unit:"%",display:"75%"},{label:"Hard savings p.a.",value:t,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(t)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Compliance becomes the path of least resistance, not the friction point. The agent meets requesters where they already work and only asks the right questions.

quote.text: Within three months of rolling out the Guided Buying Agent, people actually started coming to us. The agent made ordering feel as simple as messaging a coworker - and procurement became the path of least resistance.
quote.role: Group Chief Procurement Officer (CPO), European Business Services Group

deepDive.subtitle: What unmanaged maverick buying costs you today.

pockets.title: Maverick spend leakage
pockets.context: of negotiated savings leak via off-contract purchases.

pockets.title: Off-contract pricing premium
pockets.context: average price premium paid when employees go around catalogs and framework deals.

pockets.title: Supplier proliferation
pockets.context: of one-time vendors never go through full vetting - supplier risk and master-data debt accumulates.

deepDive.calloutTitle: Total exposure on €500M indirect spend: €15-25M / year
deepDive.calloutBody: The Guided Buying agent recaptures most of this by making the compliant path the path of least resistance.
deepDive.sourcesLine: Sources: ProcureCon (2025), APQC PCF, Hackett, EY (2025).

name: PR Review Agent
tagline: From Purchase Request (PR) to Purchase Order (PO): autonomous, compliant, no buyer touch.

intro.description: Every Monday a buyer opens 60 Purchase Requests. Half are missing a cost center, a quarter route through the wrong approval flow, some are split to dodge the approval threshold, a handful belong to a catalog the requester forgot. The buyer spends a full morning routing, fixing approver chains, and converting clean PRs to POs by hand - every week, while strategic talent gets stuck in transactional plumbing.

calculator.headline: Hours freed per week, per operational-buyer FTE

inputs.label: Current hours/week per FTE on PR review

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.current*(e.reduction/100),n=t*e.ftes;return[{label:"Hours freed per week, per FTE",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${t.toFixed(1)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per week",value:n,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per month",value:n*4.33,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*4.33)} h/mo`},{label:"Team total per year",value:n*46,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*46).toLocaleString("en-US")} h/yr`}]}

businessCase.rationale: Lio customer data: global Tier-1 manufacturer 75% → 99.6% automation in 6 months.

quote.text: The team was spending most of their time turning PRs into POs. We needed strategic capacity, not more headcount. Six months in, the agent has 99.8% execution quality - including the most complex processes.
quote.role: Chief Procurement Officer, Global Tier-1 Industrial Manufacturer

deepDive.subtitle: What rushed PR-to-PO conversion costs you today.

pockets.title: Skipped compliance checks
pockets.context: of compliance checks get shortcut by buyers under deadline pressure - non-compliance enters the pipeline silently.

pockets.title: Wrong approval routing
pockets.context: of PRs route through the wrong approval flow - delays, escalations, audit-trail gaps.

pockets.title: Maverick conversion
pockets.context: of PRs that should have been routed to catalog / framework deals get manually converted to off-contract POs.

deepDive.calloutTitle: On a €500M indirect spend base: €5-15M / year of leakage
deepDive.calloutBody: And audit findings that pile up over time.

name: Order Confirmation Agent
tagline: Chase order confirmations and resolve disputes autonomously - via email or phone.

intro.description: Send a Purchase Order (PO) Monday morning. Tuesday, nothing. Wednesday, send the chase email. Thursday, send the reminder. Friday afternoon, call the supplier. The OC finally arrives Monday with the wrong delivery date, the wrong unit price, and a quantity that doesn't match. Now the buyer reopens the negotiation thread by email, in three back-and-forth rounds, before the OC is clean. Half a buyer's week is spent on follow-ups and discrepancy emails - time that should go to category strategy and the next sourcing event. The agent chases suppliers the moment a PO goes out, parses every OC line, and resolves price/date/quantity disputes by email or phone autonomously.

calculator.headline: Hours freed per week, per operational-buyer FTE

inputs.label: Current hours/week per FTE on OC chasing

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.current*(e.reduction/100),n=t*e.ftes;return[{label:"Hours freed per week, per FTE",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${t.toFixed(1)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per week",value:n,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per month",value:n*4.33,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*4.33)} h/mo`},{label:"Team total per year",value:n*46,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*46).toLocaleString("en-US")} h/yr`}]}

businessCase.rationale: A reminder email pings the supplier inbox. An agent reads the OC when it arrives, parses prices, dates and quantities, and negotiates discrepancies in real time.

quote.text: Our buyers were spending almost a full day every week just chasing order confirmations. The agent now follows up the moment we send a PO and only escalates when something genuinely needs a human.
quote.role: Director Operational Procurement, Industrial Automation OEM

deepDive.subtitle: Every quarter without OC automation costs you buyer hours and supply reliability.

hero.subLabel: at 50 buyers × 4 hours/week chasing OCs × 12 weeks.

bullets: Up to 40% of POs go unconfirmed on time every quarter - late discovery turns into late deliveries and panic expedites.
Buyer time spent chasing instead of negotiating: ~16,000 hours per year for a 50-buyer team - strategic capacity you keep paying for but not getting.
Buyer time spent chasing instead of negotiating: ~16,000 hours per year for a 50-buyer team - strategic capacity you keep paying for but not getting.
Supply lead-time padding becomes baked into your standard processes - you stop noticing the friction, but it stays.

name: Order Bundling Agent
tagline: Find the same or similar materials scattered across your Purchase Orders - and bundle them into negotiated savings.

intro.description: Same material at Plant A €4.20, Plant B €4.85, Plant C €5.10. Three suppliers, one item, no bundling. The savings sit in plain sight on every spend report.
flow: Purchase Order (PO) base scanned across plants / Business Units (BUs)

chips.label: European Article Number (EAN)
chips.tooltip: Matches identical Stock Keeping Units across plants.

chips.label: Material number
chips.tooltip: Matches internal item codes across Business Units.

chips.label: Stock Keeping Unit (SKU)
chips.tooltip: Matches the same item under different supplier codes.

chips.label: Description text
chips.tooltip: Multilingual fuzzy match on free-text descriptions.

chips.label: STEP files
chips.tooltip: 3D geometry match for similar manufactured parts.

calculator.headline: Realized savings on bundleable spend
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.spend*.6,n=t*(e.rate/100);return[{label:"Bundleable Purchase Order volume (~60% share)",value:t,unit:"€",display:U(t)},{label:"Applied bundling rate",value:e.rate,unit:"%",display:`${e.rate}%`},{label:"Bundling savings per year",value:n,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(n)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Applied to the bundleable share identified by Lio's product-knowledge matching (typically ~60% of total Purchase Order volume). Default 8% is conservative; up to 15% reflects best-in-class bundles on commodity categories. The agent runs detection across the entire PO base, consolidates the volume, and drafts the contract action - amend an existing framework where one exists, write a new one where it doesn't. Sources: McKinsey (2024), Hackett indirect spend (2025), World Commerce & Contracting (2025).

caseStudy.clientMeta: ~8,000 employees · €250M annual Purchase Order volume · 12 plants

headline.label: Bundling savings realized, year 1 (€250M Purchase Order volume × 60% bundleable × 8%)
headline.sub: First 12 months · 240 bundling clusters identified · €150M consolidated

quote.text: We had no idea how fragmented our purchasing actually was. The agent matched our European Article Numbers and material numbers across plants and found another 240 clusters of similar materials we never connected, using product knowledge from descriptions, STEP files and material specifications. The consolidation realized €12M on roughly €150M of bundleable spend, an average 8% savings on the consolidated portion.
quote.role: Head of Category Management

name: Tracking Agent
tagline: Live delivery tracking across every carrier, with proactive delay alerts before production is hit.

intro.description: Every Monday a planner opens fifteen carrier portals, sends six 'still on schedule?' emails, and updates the status sheet by hand. A critical inbound gets stuck at customs and nobody finds out until the line manager calls because the buffer is empty. Multiply by thousands of active Purchase Orders (POs), and the portal hunt eats half a planner's week - mostly producing information nobody acts on until it's too late.

valueDrivers.label: Risk lead time
valueDrivers.sub: early warning before the line hits

calculator.headline: Hours freed per week, per planner/buyer FTE

inputs.label: Current hours/week per FTE on tracking

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.current*(e.reduction/100),n=t*e.ftes;return[{label:"Hours freed per week, per FTE",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${t.toFixed(1)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per week",value:n,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per month",value:n*4.33,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*4.33)} h/mo`},{label:"Team total per year",value:n*46,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*46).toLocaleString("en-US")} h/yr`}]}

businessCase.rationale: One incident pays for the agent. A single 8-hour line stoppage at automotive rates (~€2.3M/hr × 8 = ~€17M) typically pays a multiple of the agent fee.

headline.label: Downtime & expedite avoided
headline.sub: First 12 months · 32,000 inbound shipments · 11 prevented escalations

quote.text: In May, a critical inbound shipment got stuck at customs - nobody would have spotted it. Lio flagged it on day 3 of 9. We rerouted, paid express, kept the line running.
quote.role: Head of Supply Chain Operations, Industrial Supplier

name: Goods Receipt Agent
tagline: Scan. Match. Resolve. Mobile goods receipts matched to Purchase Orders (POs) instantly.

intro.description: Receivers on the dock don't sit at Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems. The delivery note gets scribbled on, dropped in a tray, and keyed in two days later - if at all. By then the discrepancy can't be verified, the invoice gets blocked, and 'missing Goods Receipt (GR)' becomes the single biggest source of Accounts Payable (AP) rework.

calculator.headline: Hours freed per week, per receiving/AP FTE

inputs.label: Current hours/week per FTE on GR & rework
Invoices blocked due to missing GR p.a.

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.current*(e.reduction/100),n=t*e.ftes,r=e.blocked*18;return[{label:"Hours freed per week, per FTE",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${t.toFixed(1)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per week",value:n,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per year",value:n*46,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*46).toLocaleString("en-US")} h/yr`},{label:"AP rework avoided p.a.",value:r,unit:"€",display:U(r)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Receiving teams don't sit at desktops - they're on the dock. A mobile scan + auto-match closes the loop where the work actually happens.

caseStudy.clientMeta: Specialty materials · 9,400 employees · 14 plants · 32,000 GRs p.a.

headline.label: Goods Receipt (GR) completion rate (+24 percentage points)

quote.text: Receivers used to scribble on a delivery note, drop it in a tray, and someone in the office would key it in two days later - if at all. Now they scan with their phone and the GR is in SAP before the truck has left.
quote.role: Plant Logistics Lead, Process Industry Group

name: Invoice Agent
tagline: Touchless invoice matching beyond Optical Character Recognition (OCR) - minimal false flags.

intro.description: Accounts Payable (AP) teams drown in mismatched invoices: partial deliveries, currency rounding, tax variations, supplier-side errors. Traditional OCR + rules engines flag too many false positives, and every flag becomes manual work that an AP clerk clears by hand, one supplier email at a time. The Friday close pushes into Monday, the discount window slips, and the supplier calls AP, not the buyer, for the third time this month asking where their payment is. The agent matches with context, resolves discrepancies directly with the supplier by email, and only escalates the genuine exceptions.

calculator.headline: Hours freed per week, per AP FTE

inputs.label: Current hours/week per FTE on invoice handling

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.current*(e.reduction/100),n=t*e.ftes;return[{label:"Hours freed per week, per FTE",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${t.toFixed(1)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per week",value:n,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per month",value:n*4.33,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*4.33)} h/mo`},{label:"Team total per year",value:n*46,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*46).toLocaleString("en-US")} h/yr`}]}

businessCase.rationale: OCR + rules stop at character recognition - every edge case becomes a flag a human has to clear. Consultancies aren't the answer either: same operational work, by hand, on the same outdated tools.

quote.text: We had outsourced AP to a consultancy - €3.9M a year. After eight months with Lio, 87% of invoices are touchless. The few that aren't get flagged cleanly and our existing team handles them in minutes.
quote.role: VP Finance Operations, European Retail Group

name: Agent BPO
tagline: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) executed by agents - 100% control, seconds instead of weeks.

intro.description: Traditional Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) providers and shared service centers cost millions, take months to onboard a new process, and struggle with quality and consistency. Knowledge walks out the door with attrition. Even simple SOPs sit in queues for days. You pay for headcount, not for outcomes.

calculator.headline: Cost savings vs. BPO contract
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.contractValue*.93;return[{label:"Lio cost ratio",value:7,unit:"%",display:"7%"},{label:"Annual cost savings",value:t,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(t)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Humans learn slowly and leave. Agents transfer learnings instantly across the network - a fix in one process improves every similar process. Quality climbs over time, not down with attrition. And you keep 100% control: the agent runs in your tenant, against your SOPs, with your data.

caseStudy.clientMeta: Diversified industrial · 60,000+ employees · 14 countries · €2.1B SSC scope

quote.text: We were running a 120-Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) shared service center in Eastern Europe - costing us roughly €8M a year for procurement operations. With SOPs, we saved more than €7M per year.
quote.role: Group COO, Global Industrial Group

name: Supplier Onboarding Agent
tagline: Onboard new suppliers end-to-end. The agent collects documents from public databases and supplier email, validates compliance on arrival, and monitors expiry dates. No portal logins, no chasing.

intro.description: Supplier onboarding is mostly chasing documents. Three of seven certificates arrive, then a week of silence, then the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) certificate expires before everything is in. Multiply by hundreds of suppliers per year, and category buyers spend their week on document collection, not supplier strategy, performance management, or category planning.

calculator.headline: Hours freed per week, per supplier-management FTE

inputs.label: Current hours/week per FTE on onboarding admin

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.current*(e.reduction/100),n=t*e.ftes;return[{label:"Hours freed per week, per FTE",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${t.toFixed(1)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per week",value:n,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per month",value:n*4.33,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*4.33)} h/mo`},{label:"Team total per year",value:n*46,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*46).toLocaleString("en-US")} h/yr`}]}

businessCase.rationale: Engagement rates jump from ~30% (portal) to 90%+ (email in their language) because the agent meets suppliers where they already work.

caseStudy.clientMeta: Discrete manufacturing · 28,000 employees · 4,500 new suppliers p.a.

quote.text: Onboarding used to be a four-week project per supplier. The agent now does 85% of new suppliers end-to-end in two days, including sanctions and ESG screening.
quote.role: Head of Supplier Management, Multinational Industrial Group

deepDive.subtitle: Onboarding without continuous compliance carries real risk.

pockets.title: Sanctions screening gaps
pockets.context: of mid-cap companies fail full sanctions screening across all suppliers - sanctions lists update faster than manual onboarding cycles.

pockets.title: ESG & supply-chain due diligence
pockets.context: regulatory exposure under CSRD / CSDDD per material supplier-chain compliance failure.

pockets.title: Stale supplier master data
pockets.context: of supplier master records become inaccurate within 12 months - quietly inflating risk and breaking 3-way matches.

deepDive.calloutTitle: A single missed sanctions check can carry seven-figure fines
deepDive.calloutBody: Plus reputational impact - onboarding is not a one-time event.
deepDive.sourcesLine: Sources: EY (2025), CSRD / CSDDD frameworks (2024), APQC PCF.

name: Agentic Supplier Lifecycle Management
tagline: Manage the entire supplier lifecycle, from data updates to continuous performance evaluation. Suppliers interact in their own language by email, no portal logins, no chasing.

intro.description: Supplier master data goes stale the moment onboarding ends. Bank details change, certificates expire silently, performance reviews are six months old. The portal nobody logs into is still the official channel, and supplier scorecards, if they exist, are gut feel rather than live data.

calculator.headline: Hours freed per week, per supplier-management FTE

inputs.label: Current hours/week per FTE on supplier admin

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.current*(e.reduction/100),n=t*e.ftes;return[{label:"Hours freed per week, per FTE",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${t.toFixed(1)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per week",value:n,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per month",value:n*4.33,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*4.33)} h/mo`},{label:"Team total per year",value:n*46,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*46).toLocaleString("en-US")} h/yr`}]}

businessCase.rationale: Suppliers respond to email in hours; they log into portals quarterly at best. Lio's email/NL channel achieves up to 95% engagement.

quote.text: Our portal had 25% supplier adoption at best after years of trying. We switched to Lio's agent on email and within four months 91% of suppliers were actively responding. The agent now watches financial-health signals, adverse news, sanctions lists and delivery performance every day - so we hear about a deteriorating supplier the week it starts, not at the next annual review.
quote.role: Director Supplier Operations, Diversified Manufacturing Conglomerate

deepDive.subtitle: Onboarding is the easy part. Compliance erodes the moment the supplier is live, and only continuous monitoring catches it.

pockets.title: Sanctions list updates
pockets.context: Sanctions lists update weekly. A supplier cleared at onboarding can appear on a watchlist months later, through ownership changes, geographic exposure, or new restrictions. Continuous re-screening is the only way to catch it before you transact.
pockets.source: EU sanctions framework updates, Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) enforcement data (2025).

pockets.title: Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) & Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz (LkSG) ongoing duties
pockets.context: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) and German Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz (LkSG) require continuous supply-chain due diligence, not a one-time onboarding check. Fines reach 2% of global turnover for systemic gaps.
pockets.source: EU CSDDD framework (2024), LkSG (German Supply Chain Act, 2023).

pockets.title: Expired certificates & master data
pockets.context: of supplier insurance, International Organization for Standardization (ISO) and Environmental, Social, Governance (ESG) certificates expire each year without a renewal trigger. Combined with bank-detail changes that nobody flags and performance data nobody refreshes, the supplier base quietly drifts into uninsured, non-compliant exposure.
pockets.source: APQC Process Classification Framework (PCF) supplier-data benchmarks (2025), EY supplier risk studies (2025).

deepDive.calloutTitle: A single expired certificate or missed sanctions update can trigger seven-figure fines
deepDive.calloutBody: Plus delivery stops, contract breaches and reputational impact. Supplier management is a living process, not a one-time event.
deepDive.sourcesLine: Sources: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) (2024), German Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz (LkSG) (2023), Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) / EU sanctions frameworks, EY supplier risk benchmarks (2025), APQC Process Classification Framework (PCF) (2025).

name: Strategic Sourcing Hub
tagline: One Artificial Intelligence (AI)-guided hub for the full sourcing journey - from specification to award. Agents orchestrate every step, every tool, in one place.

intro.description: Strategic sourcing events take months and live across a dozen tools. Specs are inconsistent, supplier coverage is shallow, and most savings are left on the table before the auction even starts.

calculator.headline: Hard savings on sourced spend
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.spend*.042,n=e.events*e.daysPerEvent*.65;return[{label:"Hard savings p.a.",value:t,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(t)},{label:"Days freed for strategic work",value:n,unit:"d",display:`${oC(n)} d`}]}

businessCase.rationale: More qualified bidders = real competitive tension = the mechanism behind 4.2% savings. Without supplier breadth, the savings rate collapses.

quote.text: What used to be a 5-month event from kickoff to signed contract is now 6 weeks. The hub finds suppliers we never would have invited and runs bid analysis overnight. My team spends their time on category strategy, not chasing files.
quote.role: VP Indirect Procurement, Listed Industrial Group

deepDive.subtitle: Every quarter without Strategic Sourcing Hub costs you.

hero.subLabel: at €100M annual sourced spend × 4.2% Lio savings rate ÷ 4 quarters.

bullets: 1,800 cycle-days don't shorten - every event you run by email keeps your team in tactical mode for another quarter.
Top-quartile competitors close the savings-rate gap by ~1 percentage point per year - every quarter you wait widens the disadvantage.
Supplier coverage doesn't compound - you keep RFx-ing the same 3 incumbents instead of building a 13-bidder market view.

name: Tailspend Sourcing Agent
tagline: Identifies alternative vendors, autonomously creates and sends Request for Quotations (RFQs), compares quotes, and awards the best supplier - at scale, on every tail-spend purchase.

intro.description: Tail-spend purchases get a single quote from one supplier and that's it. Even a one-vendor RFQ would beat the first-offer price. The Sourcing Agent runs 3-5 vendor RFQs on every request, autonomously.

calculator.headline: Hard savings from added competition
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.tailSpend*(e.coverage/100)*(e.rate/100);return[{label:"Hard savings p.a.",value:t,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(t)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Negotiating one quote down only goes so far. Bringing 3-5 vendors into a real RFQ creates genuine price discovery and consistently outperforms negotiation alone on the same purchase.

headline.label: Savings on previously un-sourced requests
headline.sub: First 12 months · 8,200 RFQs run · €6.3M total realized

quote.text: Before Lio, our engineers would email one supplier, accept the quote, and we'd pay it. The agent now finds three to five alternatives for every request - we're saving 11% on average on purchases that previously had no competition at all.
quote.role: Head of Operational Procurement, Industrial Equipment Manufacturer

name: Negotiation Preparation
tagline: Levels up junior buyers to senior negotiators - and helps senior buyers deliver faster, more structured arguments. Real-time insights, market benchmarks, practice calls, live counter-arguments.

intro.description: Suppliers negotiate the same contract twenty times a year - your buyer sees it once every two or three years, alone, with whatever they could pull together on Friday afternoon. Benchmarks live in three different SharePoint folders or nowhere. The cost-driver model is last year's spreadsheet from a colleague who left. The counter-argument library is the buyer's own memory. Junior buyers walk into the room and get steamrolled on terms they didn't know were negotiable; senior buyers leave up to 4% on the table because they ran out of prep hours, not because they ran out of skill. The agent prepares strategy, benchmarks, practice calls and live counter-arguments, and the same buyer walks in with eight defensible levers instead of two.

impact.label: Prep time
impact.sub: days of analyst work in hours

calculator.headline: Hours freed per week, per category-buyer FTE

inputs.label: Current hours/week per FTE on negotiation prep

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.current*(e.reduction/100),n=t*e.ftes;return[{label:"Hours freed per week, per FTE",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${t.toFixed(1)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per week",value:n,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Team total per month",value:n*4.33,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*4.33)} h/mo`},{label:"Team total per year",value:n*46,unit:"h",display:`~${Math.round(n*46).toLocaleString("en-US")} h/yr`}]}

businessCase.rationale: Seniors deliver faster, more structured arguments. Juniors are leveled up to senior-level outcomes. up to 6.5% additional savings on the same contracts - without hiring.

quote.text: Our junior buyers used to walk into negotiations and get steamrolled. After three months, the same buyers are running practice calls the night before, walking in with eight counter-arguments per term, and closing on average 1.4× the savings.
quote.role: VP Procurement, Automotive Supplier Group

name: Tailspend Negotiation Agent
tagline: Autonomously renegotiates every quote end-to-end through dynamic email conversations. Driven by data and game-theory strategies.

intro.description: Tail-spend Purchase Orders (POs) under €5k get rubber-stamped at first-offer price. The Tail-Spend Negotiation Agent challenges every quote politely, data-driven, at scale - and 7 in 10 settle without a buyer ever touching them.

calculator.headline: Hard savings on previously un-negotiated spend
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.tail*(e.coverage/100)*(e.rate/100);return[{label:"Hard savings p.a.",value:t,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(t)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Humans can't economically negotiate every €3k PO. The agent can. You're capturing savings on stuff that previously got rubber-stamped at first-offer price.

quote.text: We weren't negotiating any of our sub-€5k POs. The agent runs three to five email rounds with every supplier, anchored on real benchmarks. Suppliers respect it because it's polite and data-driven - and 7 out of 10 quotes settle without a buyer ever touching them.
quote.role: Director Indirect Procurement, Mid-cap Manufacturer

deepDive.subtitle: Every quarter rubber-stamping first-offer prices costs you.

hero.subLabel: at €50M annual tail spend × 15% Lio savings rate ÷ 4 quarters.

bullets: 100% of incoming tail-spend quotes accepted at first offer - every quarter without Lio, the leak continues at run-rate.
Suppliers learn that you don't negotiate - the anchor effect compounds, and reopening conversations later gets harder.
The 24/7 auto-close benefit doesn't accumulate - every weekend of unnegotiated POs is permanently lost savings.

name: RfX Creation Agent
tagline: From stakeholder requirements to a send-ready Request for Proposal in days, not weeks.

intro.description: A Request for Proposal eats four to six weeks: drafts, stakeholder loops, requirements that surface only when the first supplier asks. Buyers spend the week chasing - not sourcing.

impact.label: Buyer stakeholder loops
impact.sub: agent runs 5-8 loops in parallel; buyer sees only the final review

calculator.headline: Days freed per year on strategic RfP events
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const n=e.events*12;return[{label:"Days saved per event (Lio benchmark)",value:12,unit:"d",display:"12 d"},{label:"Days freed per year",value:n,unit:"d",highlight:!0,display:`~${Math.round(n).toLocaleString("en-US")} d`}]}

businessCase.rationale: RfX creation is the silent gate before sourcing savings - not the supplier side. Faster, sharper specs mean more events per year, each landing with a tighter bid file. Customers reinvest freed capacity into 2-3 additional sourcing waves, worth multiples of the labor savings. Sources: Hackett (2025), ProcureCon strategic sourcing (2024).

caseStudy.clientMeta: Plant & equipment · 8,000 employees · 60 strategic RfX p.a.

headline.label: Faster spec to send (3 wk → 4 d)

quote.text: Building an RfX used to mean three weeks of meetings, drafts and revisions. Now the agent interviews stakeholders, extracts the requirements, drafts the document - and we review it in 30 minutes. We've cut sourcing cycle time in half and added two extra sourcing waves this year without a single new hire.
quote.role: Head of Strategic Sourcing, Industrial Engineering Group

name: Supplier Selection Agent
tagline: A ranked supplier shortlist, including 40% qualified new bidders, in minutes.

intro.description: Every category starts with the same four or five known suppliers because building a fresh shortlist takes weeks. The usual suspects price like the usual suspects.

calculator.headline: Additional savings from deeper shortlists
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const n=e.spend*.021;return[{label:"Lio blended uplift (depth)",value:2.1,unit:"%",display:`${2.1}%`},{label:"Additional savings per year",value:n,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(n)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Negotiation gets you maybe 1% off a quote. A deeper shortlist gets you up to 4% off the awarded contract - three qualified incumbents and four credible challengers price differently than the same five usual suspects. The Sourcing Agent then converts the shortlist into received quotes. Sources: BCG (2024), McKinsey supplier discovery (2025), Hackett (2025).

headline.label: Hard savings uplift (5 → 13 suppliers per Request for Anything (RFx))

quote.text: We were sourcing with the same four or five suppliers per category for years. The agent now finds twelve to fifteen qualified bidders per RFx (forty percent of them new to us) and ranks them on real capability data. Our average winning bid is 3.2% lower on identical categories versus last year.
quote.role: Director Strategic Sourcing, Listed Industrial Group

name: Supplier Sourcing Agent
tagline: Every supplier interaction handled until the quotes are in - outreach, questions, reminders.

intro.description: Send eight invites, get three usable quotes by Friday. Half a sourcing buyer's week is moving emails - not running events.

calculator.headline: Team hours back per week + additional EUR from deeper quote files
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const n=11*e.ftes,o=e.spend*(1.4/100);return[{label:"Hours/week freed per FTE (Lio benchmark)",value:11,unit:"h",display:"11 h"},{label:"Team hours back per week",value:n,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${Math.round(n)} h/wk`},{label:"Additional savings per year (×1.4% blended uplift)",value:o,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(o)}]}

businessCase.rationale: A sourcing event with four received quotes is structurally different from one with nine - more quotes mean a higher chance of a price-breaker. Personalized outreach in the supplier's language, instant Q&A in context and polite chasing get the long tail to engage. Combined with Supplier Selection, the funnel goes from thin to deep, the savings rate compounds, and buyers stop spending half their week moving emails. Sources: BCG (2024), Hackett (2025), Procurement Leaders Sourcing Pulse (2024).

caseStudy.clientMeta: Specialty chemicals · 11,000 employees · 240 Request for Anything (RFx) events p.a.

headline.label: Additional EUR savings realized, year 1
headline.sub: Hard savings on previously thin bid files · €300M sourced · 70% via Lio · +2% uplift

quote.text: Supplier response rates were our biggest pain. The agent personalizes outreach in the supplier's language, answers their questions instantly in context, and chases reminders without making us look pushy. We went from 55% to 89% response rate - and the deeper quote files translated into roughly €4.2M of hard savings we would never have surfaced on thin bid files.
quote.role: Head of Category Management, Specialty Chemicals Group

name: Agentic Auctions
tagline: Auctions on every sourcing event - right format, auto-built from your tender, run to award.

intro.description: Setting up an auction takes a full day. So we auction the top fifteen categories a year and rubber-stamp the other eighty. The savings sit in plain sight.

impact.label: Setup time
impact.sub: auto-populated from the running tender (vs. 4 days manually)

calculator.headline: Additional savings via auction coverage
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const n=e.spend*.024;return[{label:"Lio blended auction uplift",value:2.4,unit:"%",display:`${2.4}%`},{label:"Additional savings per year",value:n,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(n)}]}

businessCase.rationale: The savings rate per auction (up to 7% on top of RfQ) is well established - what was always missing is coverage and right format per event. Humans set up maybe a dozen auctions per quarter and default to one format. The agent extends auctions to every event where they make economic sense and picks the format based on market dynamics. Sources: BCG (2024), McKinsey Digital Sourcing (2024), SAP Ariba auction benchmarks (2025).

quote.text: We used to run e-auctions only on our top-15 categories - everything else got an RfQ and a handshake. Now the agent reads the tender, picks the right auction format, sets it up in hours and runs it while we sleep. We doubled auction volume year-on-year and added €4.2M in savings on spend that had never seen competitive bidding pressure.
quote.role: VP Indirect Procurement, Industrial Automation Group

name: Agentic Scenario Building
tagline: Award scenarios via plain-language prompting - minutes, not analyst weekends.

intro.description: Scenario modeling is the most analyst-intensive step in sourcing - hours of Excel, or a licensed platform that needs Information Technology (IT) setup per event. The analyst weekend is gone before the award decision is even discussed.

impact.label: Scenarios iterated / event
impact.sub: vs. 2-3 manually before the analyst stops

calculator.headline: Analyst hours freed AND additional EUR from optimized awards
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const n=e.events*12,r=n/40,a=e.spend*(1.7/100);return[{label:"Hours saved per event (8 scenarios × 90 min)",value:12,unit:"h",display:"12 h"},{label:"Analyst hours freed per year",value:n,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:`~${Math.round(n).toLocaleString("en-US")} h ≈ ${r.toFixed(0)} analyst weeks`},{label:"Additional savings per year (×1.7% blended award uplift)",value:a,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(a)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Why prompting beats spreadsheets and platforms: spreadsheets break under stacked constraints. Platforms require configuration per event. The Scenario Agent removes both - plain-language prompting, multi-variable optimization in seconds, full institutional memory across events. The analyst's weekend is back, and the bid file gets ten iterations instead of two. Sources: internal Lio Scenario Agent memo (2026), Coupa/Keelvar (2025), McKinsey advanced bid analysis (2024).

headline.label: Analyst weeks freed per year (≈960 h saved)

quote.text: We used to build two or three scenarios per event in Excel because there wasn't time for more. Now the analyst describes the scenario in a sentence, the agent runs it in seconds, and we iterate ten times before lunch. The weekend is back, and the constraint logic from the last event carries into the next one automatically.
quote.role: VP Strategic Sourcing, Multi-Category Manufacturer

deepDive.subtitle: Four tiers, fifteen scenarios - what the agent handles out of the box. Recognise your own.

tiers.label: Tier 3
tiers.title: Complex
tiers.descriptor: stacked constraints, sensitivity, Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)

deepDive.footnote: Source: internal Lio Scenario Agent memo (2026).

name: Agentic Tenders
tagline: Run structured public and private tenders, including auctions, with agents handling every supplier interaction.

intro.description: Public and private tenders are heavy on documentation, rounds, and supplier coordination. Most teams either fight a legacy tendering platform or run tenders out of email and spreadsheets, losing audit trail and burning analyst weeks per event.

impact.label: Platform integration
impact.sub: connects to existing tools or runs in Lio

valueDrivers.label: Platform integration
valueDrivers.sub: connects to existing tools or runs in Lio

calculator.headline: Additional savings from structured, agent-run tenders
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.spend*.015;return[{label:"Annual spend tendered",value:e.spend,unit:"",display:U(e.spend)},{label:"Lio blended uplift",value:1.5,unit:"%",display:"1.5%"},{label:"Additional savings per year",value:t,unit:"",display:U(t),highlight:!0}]}

businessCase.rationale: Tenders and auctions are the same negotiation event in two modes. Splitting them across two tools means duplicated supplier interactions, broken audit trail, and analyst weekends consolidating the data. One platform with agents running the supplier layer collapses both into one event. Cycle reduction is additional value, captured as freed analyst weeks (~10 per €100M of tendered spend) rather than EUR savings.

headline.label: Faster tender to award
headline.sub: 12 weeks → 5 weeks with Lio · first 12 months · 80 tenders run

quote.text: We used to run tenders across three places: the public-tender portal for the formal process, email for clarifications, and Excel for bid comparison. The agent now handles every supplier interaction on one connected layer, and we have a full audit trail by default. Tender cycle dropped from 12 weeks to 5 on average, and analyst weekends are back to being weekends.
quote.role: Head of Strategic Procurement

name: Agentic Evaluation
tagline: Structured bid comparison, normalized and scored on your criteria, ready for award in minutes.

intro.description: Bids arrive in eight different formats from twelve different suppliers. Normalizing them, applying your decision criteria, and producing a defensible comparison is hours of analyst work per event. Award decisions slip because the analysis time runs out before the deadline.

calculator.headline: Analyst hours freed by automated normalization and scoring
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.events*16;return[{label:"Evaluation events per year",value:e.events,unit:"",display:`${e.events}`},{label:"Hours saved per event",value:16,unit:"h",display:"16 h"},{label:"Analyst hours freed per year",value:t,unit:"h",display:`${t.toLocaleString("en-US")} h ≈ ${Math.round(t/40)} analyst weeks`,highlight:!0}]}

businessCase.rationale: The hard part of evaluation is not the scoring - it's getting twelve heterogeneous bids into one comparable structure. The agent owns the normalization. Scoring and ranking on your criteria follow in seconds, and the output lands stakeholder-ready instead of as a spreadsheet that needs a translator.

headline.label: Faster bid normalization and scoring
headline.sub: 16 h → 8 min with Lio · first 12 months · 180 evaluations

quote.text: Bid evaluation used to be the bottleneck of every strategic event. Two days of an analyst reformatting bids into Excel, then half a day scoring them. The agent now normalizes every bid the moment it arrives, scores against our weighted criteria, and produces a stakeholder-ready comparison in minutes. We've reclaimed our analyst team for sourcing strategy.
quote.role: Director, Strategic Sourcing

name: Agentic Opportunity Assessment
tagline: Strategy-engagement depth in 1 week - on-site, mapping data, surfacing opportunities, going live with a working agent.

intro.description: Procurement transformation projects take 12+ weeks of consulting, end with a slide deck, and rarely deliver a working system. The gap between analysis and impact is the entire project.

impact.label: Delivery format
impact.sub: with your team, not a deck

calculator.headline: Annual pipeline identified in week one
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.spend*.06;return[{label:"Annual procurement spend in scope",value:e.spend,unit:"",display:U(e.spend)},{label:"Pipeline identified in assessment",value:6,unit:"%",display:"6%"},{label:"Annual value identified",value:t,unit:"",display:U(t),highlight:!0}]}

businessCase.rationale: Traditional consulting trades depth for time. Lio's assessment trades time for proof: we map your data live, surface opportunities live, match agents live, and leave one running in production. You can argue with a slide deck for months. You can't argue with an agent that's already booking savings. 6% is the conservative midpoint; engagements typically surface 4-10% of procurement spend as addressable. Year-1 realization rate: 40-60% of identified pipeline.

headline.label: Annual pipeline identified in week one
headline.sub: 1-week assessment · 1 of 5 mapped agents live by day 8

quote.text: We were three months into evaluating consulting proposals when we ran the Lio assessment instead. By the end of week one, we had a prioritized opportunity map, an agent matched to each of the top five savings levers, and one was already running in production. The agent that went live captured €420k in the first quarter alone, more than the assessment cost.
quote.role: Chief Procurement Officer

name: Contract Creation Agent
tagline: Draft, redline and finalize contracts from negotiated positions, with deviations from your standard clauses flagged automatically.

intro.description: Contract drafting is a weeks-long ping-pong between procurement, legal, and the supplier. Every non-standard clause triggers an escalation, every redline round adds days, and final signature waits behind a legal queue nobody owns.

calculator.headline: Hours freed across legal and procurement
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.contracts*6;return[{label:"Contracts per year",value:e.contracts,unit:"",display:`${e.contracts.toLocaleString("en-US")}`},{label:"Hours saved per contract",value:6,unit:"h",display:"6 h"},{label:"Hours freed per year",value:t,unit:"h",display:`${t.toLocaleString("en-US")} h`,highlight:!0}]}

businessCase.rationale: Standard clauses move quickly. Non-standard clauses trigger legal escalation, supplier counter-redlines and weeks of silence. The agent surfaces every deviation on the first draft, sets it side-by-side with your standard, and routes it to the right approver. The escalation queue stops being a queue.

headline.label: Faster contract close
headline.sub: 6 weeks → 9 days with Lio · first 12 months · 580 contracts drafted

quote.text: Our contracting bottleneck wasn't volume - it was the clause escalations. Every other contract had a non-standard term that triggered legal review, three rounds of redlining, and a two-week silence somewhere. The agent now flags every deviation on the first draft, routes it to the right approver, and we close contracts in days instead of weeks. Legal love it more than we do.
quote.role: VP Procurement Operations

name: Contract Surveillance Agent
tagline: Autonomously monitors every contract - flags when to renegotiate based on the current economic situation, and surfaces savings hidden in contracts you're already running.

intro.description: Sometimes opening a renegotiation is wrong: in a tight market you can end up with higher prices. Lio reads the current economic situation per category and flags only when conditions favor you. The agent surfaces; your team executes.

calculator.headline: Renewal + hidden savings opportunity
calculator.breakdown: e=>{const t=e.renewal*.08*.85,n=e.active*.15*.045,r=t+n;return[{rows:[{label:"Contract value renewing p.a.",value:U(e.renewal)},{label:"Renewal savings rate",value:"8%",op:"×",muted:!0},{label:"Lio renewal capture rate",value:"85%",op:"×",muted:!0}],total:{label:"Renewal Savings Opportunity",value:U(t)}},{rows:[{label:"Total active contract value",value:U(e.active)},{label:"Addressable share",value:"15%",op:"×",muted:!0},{label:"Hidden savings rate",value:"4.5%",op:"×",muted:!0}],total:{label:"Hidden Savings Opportunity",value:U(n)}},{rows:[{label:"Renewal Savings Opportunity",value:U(t)},{label:"Hidden Savings Opportunity",value:U(n),op:"+"}],total:{label:"Total Opportunity p.a.",value:U(r),highlight:!0}}]}
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.renewal*.08*.85,n=e.active*.15*.045;return[{label:"Renewal savings opportunity",value:t,unit:"€",display:U(t)},{label:"Hidden savings opportunity",value:n,unit:"€",display:U(n)},{label:"Total opportunity p.a.",value:t+n,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(t+n)}]}

businessCase.rationale: The agent surfaces opportunities - your team executes. Assumptions: 8% renewal savings rate, 85% Lio capture rate on flagged renewals, 15% of active contracts addressable, 4.5% hidden savings rate on addressable volume. Typical capture rate on identified savings: up to 70%.

quote.text: We had 1,400 active contracts and no idea which ones were quietly auto-renewing. The agent flagged 230 renewals we'd otherwise have missed - and surfaced €2.1M in unused volume rebates from one logistics partner alone.
quote.role: Head of Procurement Operations, Logistics & Transport Group

deepDive.subtitle: What unmanaged contract risk costs you today.

pockets.title: Auto-renewal lock-in
pockets.context: of renegotiation windows close unnoticed - stale prices roll forward another term.

pockets.title: Unclaimed rebates & tier discounts
pockets.context: of volume rebates never get claimed - the contracted savings don't materialise.

pockets.title: Revenue leakage from poor contract management
pockets.context: of revenue leaks through contract execution issues post-signature.

deepDive.calloutTitle: On a €1B active contract base: €20-50M / year of unrealized value
deepDive.calloutBody: Sits in contracts you've already signed.
deepDive.sourcesLine: Sources: Aberdeen (2025), Forrester / Ironclad (2025), World Commerce & Contracting (2025).

name: Spend-to-Contract Agent
tagline: Turn maverick spend into negotiated framework agreements - automatically, at scale.

intro.description: Up to 50% of indirect spend runs without a framework. Every Purchase Order is a one-off first-offer price. The maverick spend report shows the number every quarter - never the contract action.

calculator.headline: Hard savings on off-contract spend
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const n=e.spend*.0375;return[{label:"Lio blended negotiated rate",value:3.75,unit:"%",display:`${3.75}%`},{label:"Hard savings per year",value:n,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(n)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Default 7.5% savings on the addressable share (~50%) of off-contract spend. Toggle the rate up to 10% for your category mix. Procurement teams know which spend should be under contract - they don't have the bandwidth to drive contracting at scale. The agent identifies, prioritizes, drafts and negotiates in parallel across dozens of suppliers. Coverage moves by up to 30% in year 1, and the negotiated savings stick across every future Purchase Order.

headline.label: Average savings on €260M of previously maverick spend
headline.sub: 0 → €260M under contract · 73 of 80 contracting candidates closed

quote.text: 40% of our indirect spend had no framework - we knew it, we just couldn't get to it. The agent identified the top 80 contracting candidates, drafted, negotiated and closed new frameworks directly with suppliers. Average savings of 7.8% on €260M of previously maverick spend, without expanding the contracting team by a single Full-Time Equivalent (FTE).
quote.role: VP Indirect Procurement

name: Agentic Data Analytics
tagline: Data-driven insights from across your fragmented systems - live analytics and category strategy suggestions for any procurement question.

intro.description: Procurement data lives in 8+ systems. Answering a single question takes weeks of analyst work, and most savings opportunities stay hidden in data nobody has time to look at. Lio unifies everything live.

subCapabilities.title: Supplier Analysis
subCapabilities.description: Supplier base structure, performance, risk and spend exposure across the supplier portfolio.

subCapabilities.title: Contract Analysis
subCapabilities.description: Coverage, expiry calendar, renegotiation opportunities, off-contract leakage.

subCapabilities.title: Order / Spend Analysis
subCapabilities.description: Purchase Order patterns, fragmentation, bundling potential, anomalies.

calculator.headline: Additional savings opportunity identified
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.spend*(e.rate/100);return[{label:"Savings opportunity identified",value:t,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(t)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Up to 15% is the opportunity Lio surfaces - your team executes. 90% less time to insight means strategies stay live, not snapshots gathering dust.

headline.label: Material Groups with live data behind every refresh
headline.sub: Strategy refresh cadence enabled · 2 / year → on demand with Lio

quote.text: Before Lio, every category review was an Excel marathon: six weeks of pulling data from Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP), Procure-to-Pay (P2P) and three regional systems, and we did it twice a year per category. Now the agent surfaces a defensible answer in minutes, and every Material Group strategy is refreshed whenever we ask - feedstock shifts, supplier risk changes, Business Unit (BU) pivots. Strategy stopped being a yearly project and became a live workflow.
quote.role: Director, Category Management

name: Agentic Strategy Creation
tagline: A complete Material Group Strategy - Current State, Objectives, Risks, Targets - in hours, not weeks.

intro.description: A Material Group Strategy takes three to six weeks: spend data analytics, supplier landscape mapping, external market intelligence, engineering and Business Unit (BU) alignment, risk assessment, target setting. Most categories don't have a current one - they run on outdated assumptions.

impact.label: Refresh cycle
impact.sub: re-runnable for any market or business change

impact.label: Savings uplift
impact.sub: from acting on current vs. outdated assumptions

valueDrivers.label: Refresh cycle
valueDrivers.sub: re-runnable for any market or business change

valueDrivers.label: Savings uplift
valueDrivers.sub: from acting on current vs. outdated assumptions

calculator.headline: Additional savings from unified, current strategies

inputs.label: Savings uplift from unified, current strategies

calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.spend*(e.uplift/100);return[{label:"Applied uplift",value:e.uplift,unit:"%",display:`${e.uplift}%`},{label:"Additional savings per year",value:t,unit:"€",highlight:!0,display:U(t)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Why unified, current strategy wins: strategies built once and forgotten guide buyers to last year's market. When every Material Group has a current, unified strategy across the portfolio, buyers act on today's intelligence - and capture up to 4% more savings than when strategies are scattered or outdated. Sources: Strategy refresh, savings-uplift and risk-reduction benchmarks per Hackett category management studies (2025), McKinsey procurement value studies (2024).

headline.label: Days freed across the category team, year 1
headline.sub: Plus ~€2.4M consulting spend avoided · all 47 MGs covered, refreshed quarterly

quote.text: We had current strategies for twelve of our forty-seven Material Groups. The other thirty-five ran on assumptions from 2023. The agent generated full strategy documents for every MG in a week - current state, ranked risks, prioritized objectives, concrete targets - and we now refresh them quarterly instead of once a year. The category managers spend their time deciding, not assembling.
quote.role: Chief Procurement Officer, Diversified Industrial Group

name: Agentic Strategy Execution
tagline: Three specialized agents apply your category strategy to the daily procurement workflow - bundling demand, surveilling contracts and converting maverick spend into managed agreements.

intro.description: A great strategy is wasted if execution doesn't follow. Most procurement orgs have the insight - they don't have the operational lift to bundle thousands of Purchase Orders (POs), watch every contract, and chase off-contract spend in parallel. Strategy stays shelfware.

subCapabilities.title: Order Bundling Agent
subCapabilities.description: Finds where the same or similar materials are scattered across your Purchase Orders - and bundles them into negotiated savings.

subCapabilities.title: Contract Surveillance Agent
subCapabilities.description: Autonomously monitors every contract and framework agreement, proactively flags renewals, drives renegotiations, and realizes optimization potential.

subCapabilities.title: Spend-to-Contract Agent
subCapabilities.description: Turns maverick spend into negotiated framework agreements - identifies, prioritizes, drafts and negotiates new contracts at scale.

caseStudy.clientMeta: ~25,000 employees · €1.5B procurement spend · operations in 14 countries

headline.label: Blended realized rate on addressable spend
headline.sub: 0 → €78M total realized savings, year 1 · all three execution agents in production · €1B addressable spend

quote.text: Strategy used to die in PowerPoint. Now the strategy flows directly into action: the Bundling Agent identifies the consolidation moves on day one, Contract Surveillance catches the renegotiation windows we'd have missed, and the Spend-to-Contract Agent brings the long tail of maverick spend under negotiated frameworks. We realized €78M in year one across all three - measurable, traceable, on the same dashboard.
quote.role: Group Chief Procurement Officer

name: Scale your agents
tagline: Every buyer's personal Agent Builder - turn any routine task into a custom agent, in natural language, with no coding required.

intro.description: Strategic buyers lose roughly half their day to tactical work - quote comparisons, contract checks, supplier research, email drafting. Routine crowds out the strategic work they were actually hired for. Scale Your Agents gives every buyer their own building blocks: Agent Builder to turn any task into a live agent, Agentic Forms to capture structured intake, and an Agentic Performance Dashboard to see what every agent is doing.

impact.label: Routine baseline
impact.sub: the workload Lio acts on per buyer

subCapabilities.title: Agent Builder
subCapabilities.description: Turn any routine task into a custom agent - natural language, no code, live in hours.

subCapabilities.title: Agentic Forms
subCapabilities.description: Structured intake that routes requests to the right agent or workflow automatically.

subCapabilities.title: Agentic Performance Dashboard
subCapabilities.description: See what every agent is doing - adoption, hours freed, quality, exceptions.

calculator.headline: Team hours freed per week
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.buyers*4.5;return[{label:"Hours freed per buyer per week",value:4.5,unit:"h",display:"4.5 h"},{label:"Team hours freed per week",value:t,unit:"h",highlight:!0,display:rC(t)}]}

businessCase.rationale: Coding isn't the bottleneck - knowing the task is. Every buyer knows their own pain points. Agent Builder lets them automate them in natural language, with no IT ticket. By month 6, customers typically see ~1.6 custom agents built per buyer: pure compound productivity, no central rollout needed.

headline.label: Strategic time recaptured per buyer
headline.sub: Time on tactical tasks: 55% → 43% with Lio

quote.text: In the first month, our buyers built forty small agents on their own: quote checkers, supplier comparisons, contract red-flag scanners. None of it required IT. Each person automates whatever frustrates them most. We get almost an hour of every buyer's day back, and they're spending it on negotiations, not paperwork.
quote.role: Head of Indirect Procurement

name: Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs)
tagline: Hire Lio engineers to build cutting-edge Artificial Intelligence (AI) agents with your team on-site, combining advanced AI expertise with your procurement knowledge.

intro.description: First-of-kind agents and complex multi-agent workflows are hard to design from scratch. Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) combine deep AI engineering with hands-on procurement reality, and ship production-ready systems in 2-6 weeks.

calculator.headline: Engagement payback in months
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.engagementCost/e.annualValue*12,n=e.annualValue/e.engagementCost;return[{label:"Year-1 ROI",value:n,unit:"×",display:`${n.toFixed(1)}×`},{label:"Payback period",value:t,unit:"months",highlight:!0,display:`${t<1?"< 1":t.toFixed(1)} months`}]}

businessCase.rationale: Most consulting firms have AI engineering OR procurement reality, not both - and most send a generalist team that learns on your dime. Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) do neither.

caseStudy.clientMeta: Tier-1 components · 14,000 employees · First-of-kind multi-agent rollout

quote.text: Two Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) flew in for four weeks, built it alongside our category team, and shipped it. By the time they left, our buyers were running the system independently. The engagement paid for itself inside two months.
quote.role: VP Procurement Transformation, Automotive Supplier Group

name: Lio Assistant
tagline: A procurement-knowledge AI assistant: pre-trained, connected to your data, capable of reasoning and tool selection.

intro.description: Buyers do dozens of small reasoning tasks every day, comparing quotes, looking up supplier history, drafting emails, checking contract terms against precedent. Each takes minutes. Generic AI tools don't know your data, your suppliers, or your processes.

impact.label: For
impact.sub: the ones you still do by hand

valueDrivers.label: For
valueDrivers.sub: the ones still done by hand

calculator.headline: Team hours freed per week
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.buyers*3;return[{label:"Buyers in your team",value:e.buyers,unit:"",display:`${e.buyers}`},{label:"Hours freed per buyer per week",value:3,unit:"h",display:"3 h"},{label:"Team hours freed per week",value:t,unit:"h",display:`${t.toLocaleString("en-US")} h/wk`,highlight:!0}]}

businessCase.rationale: Generic AI tools answer general questions. Lio Assistant answers your questions about your data, with the procurement context - supplier relationships, contract history, sourcing precedent - already loaded. The difference between a buyer typing into a generic chatbot and a buyer asking Lio is six minutes of context-setting per task.

headline.label: Team hours freed per year (24 buyers × 50 weeks)
headline.sub: 0 → 3 h back per buyer per week · first 6 months

quote.text: Our buyers were typing the same supplier-context paragraphs into a generic chatbot before asking each question. Lio Assistant has the supplier, the contract history, and the procurement playbook already loaded - they ask one question and get an answer grounded in our data, not generic AI. Each buyer is back about three hours a week.
quote.role: Head of Procurement

name: Lio Operating Procedures
tagline: Turn Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) into Agent Operating Procedures (AOPs) - one orchestration layer across buyers, suppliers, and requesters.

intro.description: Procurement runs on Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) that live in PDFs. Every new automation re-interprets them, every integration is point-to-point, every tool is trained separately. There's no infrastructure to run agents at scale across buyers, suppliers, and requesters.

calculator.headline: Annual value from operationalized procedures
calculator.formulaSource: e=>{const t=e.sops*12e4;return[{label:"SOPs to operationalize",value:e.sops,unit:"",display:`${e.sops}`},{label:"Value per SOP automated",value:12e4,unit:"",display:"€120k"},{label:"Annual value unlocked",value:t,unit:"",display:U(t),highlight:!0}]}

businessCase.rationale: Every standalone procurement automation is a point integration: SOP-to-tool, tool-to-data, tool-to-tool. Lio Operating Procedures is the layer that turns the SOP itself into the integration. Add an agent, it inherits the infrastructure. Change a process, every agent picks it up. No re-integration project per change. Default per-SOP value covers analyst hours freed plus process-quality gains; range €40k-€250k depending on scope.

quote.text: Before, every new automation was a six-week IT project. We'd take an SOP from PDF, redocument it in BPMN, integrate it point-to-point with our Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system, train the team, and never reuse anything. Lio Operating Procedures made the SOP the infrastructure. We added 80 agentic procedures in the first year, none of them needed a fresh integration.
quote.role: Chief Procurement Officer
